descriptions: Hemophilia is a group of inherited bleeding disorders in which the blood does not clot properly.

disease: hereditary breast and ovarian cancer syndrome
descriptions: Hereditary breast and ovarian cancer syndrome is an inherited condition that significantly increases the risk of developing breast and ovarian cancers, often associated with mutations in genes like BRCA1 and BRCA2.
Many hereditary diseases are involved in pathogenesis affected by retrotransposition.

disease: hereditary neuropathy with liability to pressure palsies
descriptions: Hereditary neuropathy with liability to pressure palsies (HNPP) is an inherited peripheral neuropathy caused by misalignment between the two elements of the CMT1A-REP binary repeat on chromosome 17p11.2-p12.
High blood pressure (hypertension) is a condition in which the force of the blood against the artery walls is too high.
High-grade ovarian serous carcinoma is a type of ovarian cancer.
High-grade serous ovarian carcinoma (HGSOC) is the most common and aggressive subtype of ovarian cancer.
Human cancer is a disease characterized by uncontrolled cell growth and tumor formation.
Human diseases can be caused by mutations, including those attributable to retrotransposable elements.
Human disorders are conditions that can be caused or influenced by the mobilization of retrotransposons in the genome.
Genetic diseases that are passed down from parents to offspring.
Human lung cancer is a malignant tumor that begins in the cells of the lungs.
Human neoplasms refer to abnormal growths of tissue, which can be benign or malignant (cancerous).
Human ovarian carcinomas are malignant tumors of the ovary.
Human pathogenesis refers to the process by which a disease develops in humans.
A category of human diseases caused by the insertion of retrotransposable elements into the genome.
Human tumors are abnormal growths of tissue that can be benign or malignant (cancerous).
Hydrocephalus is a condition characterized by an abnormal accumulation of cerebrospinal fluid in the brain.
Hypercholesterolemia is a condition characterized by high levels of cholesterol in the blood.
Hypospermatogenesis is a condition characterized by reduced sperm production in the testes.
Idiopathic temporal lobe epilepsy (TLE) is a form of epilepsy with seizures originating in the temporal lobe, without a known structural cause.
Ileal carcinoid is a type of neuroendocrine tumor originating in the ileum.
Infertility is a disease characterized by the inability to achieve pregnancy after regular unprotected sexual intercourse.
Infiltrating ductal carcinoma is the most common type of invasive breast cancer.

descriptions: Inflammation is a biological response of the immune system to harmful stimuli.
Inherited disease refers to genetic disorders passed from parents to offspring.
Insertional mutagenesis is a process where the insertion of genetic material disrupts gene function, potentially leading to disease.
Intestinal-type adenocarcinoma is a common subtype of gastric cancer with glandular formation.
Kidney cancer is a type of epithelial cancer of the kidney.
Laminopathies are a group of rare genetic disorders caused by mutations in genes encoding nuclear lamins or lamin-associated proteins.
Late-onset Alzheimer's disease (LOAD) is an age-related neurodegenerative disorder.
Leukemia is a group of cancers that usually begin in the bone marrow and result in high numbers of abnormal white blood cells.
Lichen simplex chronicus (LSC) is a chronic skin condition characterized by thickened, leathery skin due to repeated scratching or rubbing.
Liver cancer is a cancer that begins in the cells of the liver.
Lung adenocarcinoma (LUAD) is a type of non-small cell lung cancer that originates in the glandular cells of the lung.
Lung cancer is a malignant tumor that begins in the cells of the lungs.
Lung carcinoma is a type of cancer that begins in the lungs.
Lung metastatic melanoma is a cancer that has spread from the skin to the lungs.
Lung neoplasia refers to the formation of new, abnormal tissue growth in the lung.
Lung Squamous Cell Carcinoma.
Major depression is a common and serious mood disorder that causes severe symptoms affecting how you feel, think, and handle daily activities.
Major depression disorder is a neurological disorder.
Major depressive disorder (MDD) is a neurological disorder.
The presence of cancerous cells that have the potential to invade and spread.
Malignant transformation is the process by which cells acquire the properties of cancer, such as uncontrolled growth, invasion, and metastasis.
Mammalian aging refers to the complex biological process of functional decline over time in mammals.
Melanoma is a type of skin cancer that develops from pigment-producing cells known as melanocytes.
Mental disorders are a broad category of illnesses that affect mood, thinking, and behavior.
Metabolic disease refers to a class of disorders affecting the body's metabolism.
Metastatic peritoneal carcinoma refers to cancer that has spread to the peritoneum, the lining of the abdominal cavity.
Microcephaly is a neurodevelopmental disorder characterized by a smaller than average head size.
The Major Histocompatibility Complex (MHC) class I region is strongly associated with more than 100 different diseases.
Multiple myeloma is a cancer of plasma cells, a type of white blood cell.
Muscle-invasive bladder cancer (MIBC) is an aggressive form of bladder cancer.
A group of genetic diseases characterized by progressive weakness and loss of muscle mass.
Myelodysplastic syndromes are a group of disorders caused by poorly formed or dysfunctional blood cells.
Myeloid leukemia is a type of leukemia affecting myeloid cells.
Myeloid malignancies are a group of cancers that affect the blood, bone marrow, and lymph nodes, originating from myeloid cells.
Myeloid tumors are cancers arising from myeloid cells in the bone marrow.
Myeloma is a cancer of plasma cells, a type of white blood cell.
Neoplasia refers to the process of abnormal and uncontrolled cell growth, which can lead to the formation of a neoplasm or tumor.
Neural tube defect (NTD) is a congenital malformation of the brain and spinal cord.
Neuroblastoma is a type of cancer that develops from immature nerve cells.
Neurodegeneration is the progressive loss of structure or function of neurons, including neuronal death.

disease: neurodegenerative diseases of the motor neuron
descriptions: A group of diseases characterized by the progressive degeneration of motor neurons.
Neurodevelopmental disorders are a group of conditions with onset in the developmental period, characterized by deficits in personal, social, academic, or occupational functioning.
Neurofibromatosis is a genetic disorder that causes tumors to form on nerve tissue.
Disorders affecting the brain, spinal cord, and nerves.
Neurological diseases are disorders that affect the brain, spinal cord, and nerves.
Neurological disorders are diseases of the central and peripheral nervous system.
Neuropsychiatric disorders are a class of mental disorders that involve disturbances in brain function affecting emotion, learning, memory, and behavior.
Neutral lipid storage disease is a disorder characterized by the abnormal accumulation of triglycerides in cells.

Oncogenesis is the process of tumor formation, often involving genetic alterations.
A broad category of diseases involving abnormal cell growth with the potential to invade or spread to other parts of the body.
Ovarian cancer is a type of cancer that forms in the ovaries.
Ovarian immature teratoma is a type of germ cell tumor of the ovary.
Ovarian malignancies are cancers originating in the ovaries.
A type of cancer that forms in the tissues of the ovary.
Pancreatic cancer is a disease in which malignant (cancerous) cells form in the tissues of the pancreas.

Pancreatic neuroendocrine tumor is a rare type of cancer that forms in the hormone-producing cells of the pancreas.
Pediatric germ cell tumors are a type of cancer that occurs in children, including testicular, ovarian, and extragonadal tumors.
Pediatric malignant germ cell tumor (MGCT) is a type of cancer that arises from germ cells in children.

Premature aging syndromes are genetic disorders characterized by accelerated aging phenotypes.

Primary carcinomas are malignant tumors originating from epithelial cells in various tissues.
Progressive supranuclear palsy is a neurodegenerative disease and a type of tauopathy.
Prostate cancer is a cancer that occurs in the prostate gland.

A type of cancer that forms in the tissues of the prostate gland.
A broad category of mental health conditions that affect mood, thinking, and behavior.
Psychological disorders are mental health conditions characterized by abnormal thoughts, feelings, and behaviors.
Pyruvate dehydrogenase complex (PDHc) deficiency is a genetic disorder affecting energy metabolism.
Radiation-associated tumors are neoplasms that develop as a consequence of prior radiation therapy.

descriptions: Refractory Philadelphia chromosome-positive acute lymphoblastic leukemia refers to Ph(+)ALL that is resistant to standard treatments, including tyrosine kinase inhibitors.

Repeat expansion diseases are a class of disorders characterized by the instability of causative repeat mutations.
A group of eye diseases characterized by the progressive loss of retinal cells.

Schizophrenia is a chronic mental disorder characterized by distortions in thinking, perception, emotions, language, sense of self, and behavior.
Secondary malignancies are cancers that develop in patients exposed to genotoxic agents, such as during cancer therapy.
A class of diseases associated with cellular senescence.
Precursor lesions found in the fallopian tubes, often associated with high-grade serous ovarian cancer.

top_categories: 03 Diseases of the blood or blood-forming organs
descriptions: Sickle cell disease is a genetic blood disorder characterized by abnormal hemoglobin and sickle-shaped red blood cells.
Monogenic diseases are disorders caused by mutations in a single gene.
Skin cancer is a disease characterized by the uncontrolled growth of abnormal skin cells.
Skin tumors induced by 7,12-dimethylbenz[a]anthracene (DMBA) plus 12-O-tetradecanoylphorbol-13-acetate (TPA) in a multistep carcinogenesis model.
Solid cancers are a broad category of malignancies characterized by the formation of solid tumors, as opposed to liquid cancers like leukemias.

Sporadic diseases are caused by LINE-1 insertion into new genomic loci.
Sporadic genetic diseases are genetic disorders that occur in individuals with no family history of the condition, often due to de novo mutations.
Squamous cell carcinoma is a subtype of non-small cell lung cancer.

Tumors originating in the stomach.
Systemic autoimmune diseases are conditions where the immune system mistakenly attacks the body's own tissues.
Teratoma is a type of tumor that can contain several different types of tissue.
Testicular germ cell tumors are cancers that develop in the testicles.

Thyroid cancer is a cancer that starts in the thyroid gland.
Tonsillar carcinoma is a type of cancer that originates in the tonsils.
Transitional cell carcinoma (TCC) is a type of cancer that arises from the transitional epithelium, commonly found in the bladder.
Tuberous sclerosis is a neurodevelopmental disorder.
Tumor refers to an abnormal mass of tissue resulting from uncontrolled cell growth.

Tumor progression refers to the process by which a tumor grows, invades surrounding tissues, and potentially metastasizes.

Type 2 diabetes mellitus (T2DM) is a chronic metabolic disorder characterized by insulin resistance and relative insulin deficiency.

Upper gastrointestinal cancer includes esophageal and gastric cancers.
Uveal melanoma is a type of cancer that forms in the eye's uvea.
Various severe diseases can be caused by LINE-1 retrotransposition.
Well-differentiated liposarcoma is the most common subtype of liposarcoma, a soft tissue sarcoma.

descriptions: Yolk sac tumor is a type of malignant germ cell tumor.

top_categories: 03 Diseases of the blood or blood-forming organs
descriptions: β-thalassemia is one of the most common genetic diseases worldwide, caused by mutations in the human hemoglobin beta (HBB) gene.
